commit.message:
feat: DSH-015 React/Vite provisions (#221)

* fix(session): SES-010B cleanup — live status, wrapper audit, prune-smoke

Item 1: status subcommand now queries the live daemon
(/api/v1/dashboard/active-sessions) for session_id and agent_id,
falling back to current.env only when the daemon is unreachable.
This prevents stale env-file values from being displayed.

Item 2: qoder and kiro wrappers no longer synthesize session ids
(CODERO_SESSION_ID). They pass through CODERO_AGENT_SESSION_ID /
CODERO_SESSION_ID if already set. Added --help/--version bypass to
qoder and kilo (kiro and oc-codero already had it).

Item 3: Added prune-smoke subcommand that deletes smoke-test rows
(session_id LIKE 'agent-sess-smoke-%') from the pilot DB.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(launchers): address CodeRabbit review on PR #218

Four fixes from CodeRabbit review:

1. codero-opencode-pilot status: filter active sessions by current session id
   (SESSION_ID / CODERO_SESSION_ID) before falling back to first row; prevents
   status from printing an unrelated session's identity when daemon tracks
   multiple sessions.

2. kilo: move resolve_kilo_runtime() and binary resolution before
   agent_launch_init so the --help/--version fast-path can exec the real
   binary without going through Codero session registration. Removes the old
   post-init bypass.

3+4. agent-launch-bootstrap: remove UUID synthesis from
   agent_launch_bootstrap_codero_session(); under the SES-010 contract session
   ids must come from the agent, not be minted by the wrapper. When neither
   CODERO_AGENT_SESSION_ID nor CODERO_SESSION_ID is set, skip registration
   silently (policy=auto) or warn and skip (policy=required) instead of
   generating a random id. CODERO_AGENT_SESSION_ID is now preferred over
   CODERO_SESSION_ID when both are set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kilo): defer workdir resolution until after help/version fast-path

kilo --help / --version was still going through agent_launch_resolve_workdir
(which can fail if the resolve-agent-workdir tool is absent or AGENT_REPO_DIR
is not set) before reaching the bypass. Move workdir resolution and
agent_launch_init to after the help/version check so help output is truly
side-effect-free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: DSH-015 React/Vite provisions - add UI scaffolding

* fix(dashboard): bump Vite to v5, add TypeScript devDep, extend ESLint to TS

Address CodeRabbit feedback on PR #221:
- Upgrade vite from ^4.4.5 to ^5.0.0 per DSH-015 ADR requirement
- Add typescript ^5 as devDependency
- Extend lint script to cover ts,tsx file extensions

* fix(dsh-015): guard kilo runtime resolution against set -e early exit

Replace direct command substitution with conditional assignment so that
resolve_kilo_runtime failure under set -euo pipefail reaches the controlled
exit 127 path instead of immediately terminating the shell.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: self-referencing childIds cycle corrupts gear export for nested items (SINs/licenses) (#91)

fix: correct updateListLinks childIds self-reference bug + add gearToTree tests

`updateListLinks` was pushing `savedItem.id` (the parent's own id) instead of
`updatedItem.id` (the child's id) when linking nested gear items. This caused
a self-referencing cycle in `childIds` whenever a license was added to a SIN
through the builder UI, making `gearToTree` infinitely recurse and crash the
export.

Fix: change `savedItem.childIds.push(savedItem.id)` →
     `savedItem.childIds.push(updatedItem.id)` in useGearApi.ts

Added 9 unit tests in exportUtils.test.ts covering:
- empty gear
- root-only SINs (no children)
- multiple root SINs
- SIN with one nested license
- SIN with multiple nested licenses
- mix of SINs with and without licenses
- parentId/childIds are omitted from every output node
- child items do not appear at root level
- items without childIds field (legacy data)

* fix(web): SharedWebGLContext viewport ghost pixels and stale glyph data

Two bugs in SharedWebGLContext:

1. setViewport() didn't invalidate the terminal, so stale pixels
   persisted after hiding a pane (viewport set to 0,0,0,0). Fix:
   delete from terminalFullyRendered on viewport change. Fixes #135.

2. rowGlyphCounts and rowBgCounts initialized to 0 instead of cols
   (same bug as #127 in standalone WebGLRenderer). Stale glyph data
   wasn't cleared on first render after re-add or resize. Fixes #136.

Added 2 SharedWebGLContext integration tests:
- Both panes render independently with content verification
- Heavy parallel writes to all panes

Total: 24 Playwright integration tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add SharedWebGLContext regression tests for #135 and #136

4 unit tests verifying:
- setViewport invalidates dirty tracking (catches #135 ghost pixels)
- setViewport hide→show cycle triggers re-render
- Re-add terminal after remove rebuilds row tracking (catches #136)
- updateTerminal with different dimensions rebuilds tracking

These would have caught both bugs before they shipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings in getRowTexts and TerminalHandle

1. getRowTexts() now treats codepoint 0x00 as space (was silently
   dropped, causing "A" + empty + "B" → "AB" instead of "A B")
2. getRowTexts() explicitly skips wide-char spacer cells via
   isSpacerCell() instead of relying on codepoint 0 coincidence

Castellarius: comprehensive state machine simplification (#422)

## Summary

Accumulated hardening fixes had turned the Castellarius into a
non-deterministic mess that could burn LLM tokens in respawn loops and
other edge cases. This PR strips all of that back to the clean
invariants.

## Invariants restored

1. Only agents write outcomes - Castellarius never calls SetOutcome
2. Castellarius never kills sessions - if tmux is alive, let it run
3. Every spawn is always fresh - no --continue/resume logic
4. Sessions exit automatically - exec prefix + remain-on-exit off make
zombie state structurally impossible

## Changes

### Deleted entirely
- dispatch_loop.go + tests - race-prone heuristic recovery loop
- stuck_delivery.go + tests - invasive session killing mechanism

### scheduler.go
- Removed phantom commit check (false positives)
- Removed auto-promote (masked misbehaviour)
- Removed dirty worktree redundant checks
- Removed dirtyNonContextFiles(), sandboxHead(), GetLastReviewedCommit
- Removed dispatchLoop, rebaseAndPushFn, ghMergeFn, findPRFn,
killSessionFn struct fields

### session.go (cataractae)
- Removed priorSessionCount(), buildContinueCmd(), --continue flag -
always fresh spawn
- Prepend exec to agent command: shell replaced by agent, pane closes
when agent exits
- Set remain-on-exit off after session creation to override any user
tmux.conf
- Deleted isAgentAlive() and tmuxDisplayMessage - zombie state is now
structurally impossible

### Tests
- Fixed 2 flaky timing tests using waitCalls instead of time.Sleep(50ms)
- Updated all HasPrefix assertions for exec prefix
- Deleted TestIsAgentAlive_* tests (5 tests)
